questions.q: In which year did Narendra Modi first become Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Narendra Modi became Prime Minister of India in 2014.

questions.q: Before becoming Prime Minister, Narendra Modi served as the Chief Minister of which state?
questions.explanation: He previously served as Chief Minister of Gujarat for over a decade.

questions.q: In which years was Narendra Modi re-elected as Prime Minister after his first term?
questions.explanation: He was re-elected as Prime Minister in 2019 and again in 2024.

questions.q: Whom did Narendra Modi succeed as Prime Minister in 2014?
questions.explanation: He succeeded Manmohan Singh as Prime Minister in 2014.

questions.q: Narendra Modi leads which coalition as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: He leads the National Democratic Alliance (NDA) as Prime Minister.

questions.q: Counting his electoral victories, how many times had Narendra Modi been elected to lead a Union government as of 2024?
questions.explanation: As of 2024 he had been elected to lead the Union government three times, in 2014, 2019 and 2024.

questions.q: Narendra Modi's tenure as Prime Minister began following the general election to which Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: He first became Prime Minister after the 2014 election that constituted the 16th Lok Sabha.

questions.q: Narendra Modi served as Chief Minister of Gujarat for approximately how long before becoming Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: He served as Chief Minister of Gujarat for over a decade before becoming Prime Minister.

questions.q: As Prime Minister, Narendra Modi heads which branch of the Union government?
questions.explanation: As Prime Minister he heads the executive branch of the Union government.

questions.q: Narendra Modi is a leader of which political party?
questions.explanation: He is a senior leader of the Bharatiya Janata Party, which leads the NDA.

questions.q: On which date in 2014 did Narendra Modi first take the oath of office as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: He was sworn in as Prime Minister on 26 May 2014.

questions.q: Narendra Modi represents which Lok Sabha constituency since 2014?
questions.explanation: He has represented the Varanasi constituency in the Lok Sabha since 2014.

questions.q: Which President of India administered the oath of office to Narendra Modi when he first became Prime Minister in 2014?
questions.explanation: Pranab Mukherjee was the President of India in 2014 when Modi was sworn in.

questions.q: Narendra Modi first became Chief Minister of Gujarat in which year?
questions.explanation: He first became Chief Minister of Gujarat in 2001.

questions.q: The Goods and Services Tax was introduced on 1 July 2017 during Narendra Modi's tenure. It came through which constitutional amendment?
questions.explanation: The GST was introduced through the 101st Constitutional Amendment during his tenure.

questions.q: The state of Telangana was formed in June 2014, around the time Narendra Modi took office. It became which numbered state of India?
questions.explanation: Telangana was formed on 2 June 2014 as the 29th state of India.

questions.q: Narendra Modi's second term as Prime Minister began after the general election to which Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: His second term began in 2019 after the election that constituted the 17th Lok Sabha.

questions.q: A new Parliament building was inaugurated in 2023 during Narendra Modi's tenure, replacing the role of the building inaugurated in which year?
questions.explanation: The new Parliament building was inaugurated in 2023, succeeding the older building that had been inaugurated in 1927.

questions.q: Narendra Modi was sworn in for his third term as Prime Minister in which month and year?
questions.explanation: He was sworn in for his third term in June 2024 following that year's general election.

questions.q: Narendra Modi's tenure as Prime Minister belongs to which phase of national politics characterised by alliances such as the NDA?
options: The era of coalitions and alliances
questions.explanation: His tenure falls within the era of coalitions, leading the NDA alliance.

questions.q: As Prime Minister, Narendra Modi serves as the chairperson of which policy think tank established in 2015?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister serves as the chairperson of NITI Aayog, established in 2015 during his tenure.

questions.q: The Planning Commission was replaced by NITI Aayog in 2015 during Narendra Modi's first term. NITI Aayog is described as fostering what?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog, established in 2015, is described as fostering cooperative federalism.

questions.q: Narendra Modi first took office as Chief Minister of Gujarat in which month and year?
questions.explanation: He first took office as Chief Minister of Gujarat in October 2001.

questions.q: In the 2014 general election, Narendra Modi contested and won from two Lok Sabha constituencies, Varanasi and which other?
questions.explanation: In 2014 he won from both Varanasi and Vadodara, later retaining Varanasi.

questions.q: Narendra Modi was sworn in for his second term as Prime Minister on which date in 2019?
questions.explanation: He took the oath for his second term on 30 May 2019.

questions.q: Which constitutional article governs the appointment of the Prime Minister, the office Narendra Modi has held since 2014?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is appointed by the President under Article 75, the office Modi assumed in 2014.

questions.q: The new Parliament building inaugurated in 2023 during Modi's tenure houses a sceptre placed near the Speaker's chair known by which name?
questions.explanation: The ceremonial sceptre, known as the Sengol, was installed in the new Parliament inaugurated in 2023.

questions.q: Under the Constitution, the Prime Minister and other ministers hold office during the pleasure of which authority?
options: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: Under Article 75, the Prime Minister and other ministers hold office during the pleasure of the President of India.

questions.q: Before becoming Chief Minister, Narendra Modi had been a long-time organiser in which kind of political role rather than an elected one?
questions.explanation: He worked as a party organiser for many years before first taking elected office as Chief Minister in 2001.

questions.q: As of his 2024 swearing-in, Narendra Modi's continuous tenure as Prime Minister had begun in which year, making it among the longest unbroken tenures in the office?
questions.explanation: His continuous tenure as Prime Minister began in 2014 and extended through successive terms.
